title: Ireland Tightens Crypto Safeguards in New Financial Crime Action Plan
summary: Ireland has identified crypto-asset misuse as a top threat in its new National Risk Assessment. A 30-point action plan aims to tighten checks on crypto funds to combat financial crime.
body:
Ireland has announced a new 30-point action plan to strengthen safeguards against financial crime, specifically targeting crypto-asset misuse. The plan follows the country's latest National Risk Assessment, which names crypto as one of the top threats to financial stability and security.

The action plan includes measures to enhance monitoring and reporting of suspicious activities involving crypto funds. It also aims to improve cooperation between financial regulators and law enforcement agencies to better track and prevent illicit transactions. The Irish government has emphasized the need for robust measures to protect its financial system from emerging threats.

For everyday users, these measures could mean stricter know-your-customer (KYC) procedures and increased scrutiny on crypto transactions. While this may add an extra layer of security, it could also mean more hurdles for those looking to use crypto services in Ireland. The plan is part of a broader effort to align Ireland's financial regulations with international standards.

Users in Ireland should expect to see more stringent verification processes when using crypto exchanges and services. The implementation of these measures will likely be phased in over the coming months, so staying informed about the specific changes will be crucial. The plan underscores the global trend of tightening regulations around crypto to prevent financial crime.
twitterThread: Ireland just named crypto-asset misuse a top threat in its new financial crime plan. Expect stricter checks on crypto funds soon.
The 30-point plan includes enhanced monitoring and reporting of suspicious crypto transactions. It's part of a global push to tighten regulations.
